title: Architecture Improvements — Sequencing & Dependency Map
body:
## Issue description

This issue provides the recommended sequencing and dependency map for the architecture improvement epics and their child issues. It serves as a roadmap for prioritizing work across multiple PRs.

## Sequencing overview

### 🟢 Immediate (no dependencies, high impact)

These issues can be started right away in parallel:

| # | Issue | Epic | Why immediate |
|---|-------|------|---------------|
| 1 | Extract ContentDefinition types from GameTheme | Content Separation | Foundational refactor, unblocks versioning |
| 2 | Persist idempotency command results alongside keys | Persistence & Reliability | Correctness fix, standalone |
| 3 | Decompose PlayerAccount into separate persistence aggregates | Persistence & Reliability | Reduces contention, unblocks read models |
| 4 | Split API routes into command and query modules | CQRS | Structural refactor, unblocks read models |
| 5 | Add authentication middleware with token validation | Auth & Trust | Security foundation, standalone |

### 🟡 Next (depends on Immediate tier)

| # | Issue | Epic | Depends on |
|---|-------|------|------------|
| 6 | Add content version stamping | Content Separation | #1 Extract ContentDefinition |
| 7 | Bind player accounts to content version at creation | Content Separation | #1 + #6 |
| 8 | Implement transactional outbox for Service Bus | Persistence & Reliability | #3 Decompose PlayerAccount (soft) |
| 9 | Introduce read-model projection for player state | CQRS | #4 Split routes + #3 Decompose (soft) |
| 10 | Enforce trust boundaries between API, Worker, Service Bus | Auth & Trust | #5 Auth middleware |

### 🔵 Later (depends on Next tier)

| # | Issue | Epic | Depends on |
|---|-------|------|------------|
| 11 | Add leaderboard / social read model | CQRS | #9 Player read model |
| 12 | Evolve LiveEvent into a campaign system | Live Ops & Rules | #1 + #6 + #7 Content separation |
| 13 | Formalize rules engine boundaries | Live Ops & Rules | #1 + #6 Content separation |

## Dependency graph

```
Immediate:  [1] ContentDef    [2] Idempotency   [3] Split Aggregate  [4] CQRS Routes  [5] Auth
               │                                        │                  │               │
Next:          ├─► [6] Versioning                       │soft              │               │
               │      │                                 ▼                  ▼               │
               │      ├─► [7] Player Binding    [8] Outbox       [9] Read Model           │
               │      │                                                    │               ▼
Later:         │      ├─► [12] Campaigns                          [11] Leaderboard   [10] Trust
               │      └─► [13] Rules Engine
               │              ▲
               └──────────────┘
```

## Recommended parallel work streams

- **Stream A (Content):** #1 → #6 → #7 → #12, #13
- **Stream B (Persistence):** #2 + #3 (parallel) → #8
- **Stream C (CQRS):** #4 → #9 → #11
- **Stream D (Security):** #5 → #10

Streams A-D can proceed in parallel with different contributors.

## Additional notes

- All issues maintain the existing Azure platform choices (PostgreSQL, Service Bus, Container Apps, Durable Functions).
- Each issue is scoped to a single practical PR.
- Soft dependencies mean the issue benefits from but does not strictly require the dependency.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] All child issues are created and linked to their respective epics
- [ ] Work proceeds in the recommended sequence
- [ ] No circular dependencies between issues
